evidence_quotes.quote: tricks, such as dark patterns, are used to exploit fundamental human psychological weaknesses

paragraphs.text: Hello, I strongly agree with the objectives of this initiative. Specifically related to video games. I personally play video games since I was 6 years old and received a gameboy. I have also worked in the sector, as a programmer, on Indie and AAA games for 5 years. A strong trend in industry is to incorporate glueck game elements into video games. In so doing, I explicitly refer to elements in which real money can be used to participate in random games of chance in practically uncapped terms. These take the form of Counter Strike Weapons Skins, FIFA items, Lootboxes and many others. In particular, so-called Gachas are a very growing trend. In many of these cases, the concept of a video game is used to circumvent glueck game regulation. In addition, a number of tricks, such as dark patterns, are used to exploit fundamental human psychological weaknesses and ultimately exploit a gluck game grease. This strategy is unreliably profitable and more and more games of this kind are being developed and published. Genshin Impact, Honkai Star Rail, Love and Deepspace, Wuthering Waves, Infinity Nikki, ZZZ and many more. I would like to ask the initiative to regulate these games according to their nature, like all other games of chance. In essence, impact is no different from a slot machine or roulette as a business strategy. It is hidden only under the cover of an open world JRPG. There is a strong popular desire to regulate destructive trends in the video games industry. The Stop Destroying Videogames Citizen’s Initiative shows this with over 1.4 million signatures. This is about receiving video games, but I see it as having a strong overlap with this initiative. Video games are a young industry and therefore still relatively unregulated. Anti-consumption trends are starting to shape, and it is increasingly closer to a future in video games, consumers try to exploit them and then are destroyed by the manufacturer or distributor, and in extreme cases they are lost forever. Video games are a gigantic global industry and require strong regulation to make real changes. The EU is one of the few institutions in the world that have the will and the means to achieve this. Especially in the current times, exploitation as a consumer is increasingly felt and the arbitrariness of Giigant groups. On the other hand, it is important to stand up in order to regain the confidence of the people.
